Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input Database schema PostgreSQL claims adjusters claim_events temporal profile model with anomalies prompt requirements Task 1 Identify anomalies 2 Generate hypotheses for why they exist 3 Propose verification approaches using SQL queries Constraint Do not reference the instructions or explanations above Present the findings independently

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Task 1 Identify Anomalies in the Temporal Profile Model R P AVG 90000s 25h STDEV 3600s 1h Anomaly Extremely low standard deviation relative to the long process path R A E P It suggests a highly rigid artificial schedule P N AVG 604800s 7 days STDEV 172800s 2 days Anomaly Unusually long delay between approval and notifying the customer A C AVG 7200s 2h STDEV 3600s 1h Anomaly Very short time from assignment to closure likely skipping Evaluation E and Approval P steps E N AVG 300s 5m STDEV 60s 1m Anomaly Extremely fast transition from Evaluation to Notify possibly skipping Approval P

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Task 2 Generate Hypotheses R to P Rigid Approval Automated batch processing running exactly on a daily schedule or robotic process automation with fixed timers P to N Long Delay Notification is done manually via a third party mailing system or call center that operates on a weekly schedule Alternatively a backlog in the notification queue A to C Premature Closure Adjusters might be closing claims to meet SLA targets without actually evaluating them or these are duplicate spam claims that get instantly rejected and closed E to N Instant Notification The notification step is prematurely triggered by the evaluation system or N is mistakenly used as an internal Notify Adjuster rather than Notify Customer leading to immediate timestamps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task 3 Propose Verification Approaches using SQL Queries Query 1 Claims closed quickly after assignment A to C anomaly Check if E or P steps exist Query 2 Claims with rigid R to P timing Find claims where the time between R and P is exactly around 25 hours Query 3 Claims with long delays between P and N Correlate with adjuster specialization region or claim type to see if it is localized Query 4 Quick E to N transitions Identify claims where P is missing between E and N

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft the Output Section 1 Anomalies Identified Detail the 4 points Section 2 Hypotheses Provide reasons based on system human or process factors Section 3 SQL Verification Queries Provide PostgreSQL queries with CTEs to find these specific anomalous claims and correlate with claims and adjusters tables

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine SQL Queries Query A A to C premature closure SELECT c claim_id c claim_type EXTRACT EPOCH FROM c_close timestamp c_assign timestamp AS time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events c_assign ON c claim_id c_assign claim_id AND c_assign activity A JOIN claim_events c_close ON c claim_id c_close claim_id AND c_close activity C WHERE EXTRACT EPOCH FROM c_close timestamp c_assign timestamp <= 7200 AND NOT EXISTS SELECT 1 FROM claim_events e WHERE e claim_id c claim_id AND e activity IN E P Query B Long P to N delay with Adjuster Region correlation SELECT a region a specialization c claim_type AVG EXTRACT EPOCH FROM n timestamp p timestamp AS avg_delay_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events p ON c claim_id p claim_id AND p activity P JOIN claim_events n ON c claim_id n claim_id AND n activity N JOIN adjusters a ON p resource INTEGER a adjuster_id Assuming resource is adjuster_id or just join on name if resource is name Lets assume resource relates to adjuster name id WHERE n timestamp > p timestamp GROUP BY a region a specialization c claim_type Query C Rigid R to P SELECT c claim_id c claim_type c submission_date EXTRACT EPOCH FROM p timestamp r timestamp AS time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events r ON c claim_id r claim_id AND r activity R JOIN claim_events p ON c claim_id p claim_id AND p activity P WHERE EXTRACT EPOCH FROM p timestamp r timestamp BETWEEN 86400 AND 93600

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure no references to the prompt instructions Structure clearly with headings
